title: Sindhu Advances to Japan Open Semifinal After Okuhara Withdrawal
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu reaching a Super 750 semifinal is a significant achievement for Indian badminton.
summary: PV Sindhu secured her spot in the Japan Open semifinals following the withdrawal of her opponent, Nozomi Okuhara, setting up a clash with Chen Yufei.

title: Sindhu Dominates Han Yue, Shows Strength for New 15-Point Format
indian_angle: This report highlights PV Sindhu's dominant performance and discusses how the new 15-point scoring system could benefit Indian shuttlers.
summary: PV Sindhu delivered a commanding performance against Han Yue, winning 21-16, 21-14, with the article suggesting her style is well-suited for the proposed 15-point format.

title: Player Eligibility Announced for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: This circular outlines the crucial eligibility criteria for Indian junior players aspiring to represent the nation at the World Junior Championships.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has released a circular detailing the player eligibility requirements for selection to the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, to be held in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Delhi World Championships 2026: Engaging New Generations of Fans
indian_angle: With India hosting the BWF World Championships in Delhi, this article discusses the crucial need to attract younger Indian audiences to the sport.
summary: An analysis of the upcoming BWF World Championships in Delhi emphasizes the importance of attracting Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences to ensure the sport's long-term popularity in India.

title: BAI President Sarma Comments on Wildlife at India Open
indian_angle: The statement from BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma addresses a unique incident at a major Indian badminton event, providing a local perspective.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma humorously responded to an incident involving monkeys and pigeons at the India Open, stating there's no issue if they come to watch.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This marks a major change in scoring for all domestic tournaments in India, impacting how young Indian shuttlers develop their game.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) is implementing a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships (U-13, U-15 & U-17), set to debut in Ernakulam.
